changes: Enhanced refresh_columns() to intelligently preserve selection state across filesystem refreshes, clamping to valid bounds
Added update_collapse() to mark empty columns as collapsed, called after every refresh and mutation
Implemented fallback logic to find nearest non-collapsed column when focused column becomes empty
file_insights.key_logic: Selection preservation with bounds-clamping; collapsed state tracking and navigation recovery

changes: Created new 451-line module with dispatcher-based key handling by Mode
Implemented Normal mode navigation: h/l moves column focus (wrapping, skipping collapsed); j/k moves card selection (wrapping within column)
Added comprehensive test suite with 13 test cases covering edge cases: wrapping, collapsed column skipping, empty columns, arrow key fallbacks
file_insights.key_logic: rem_euclid() for wrapping with negative steps; dual iteration strategies for collapsed column skipping; ListState integration

changes: Wire input module and KanbanWidget into render loop (details truncated in diff)

patterns_discovered.description: handle_key() dispatches to mode-specific handlers; placeholder handlers for unimplemented modes (Filter, ActionPopup, etc.) allow incremental wiring without crashes
patterns_discovered.benefit: Scalable architecture for multi-mode input — can add modes without touching dispatcher

patterns_discovered.description: rem_euclid((idx + step).rem_euclid(len)) for circular navigation; loop-based skipping of collapsed columns with 'all collapsed' fallback
patterns_discovered.benefit: Handles edge cases (negative wraps, exhausted non-collapsed columns) cleanly without branching

patterns_discovered.description: refresh_columns() clamped old selection indices to new column bounds; update_collapse() called unconditionally after refresh
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents phantom selection indices when cards are deleted; keeps column-state synchronized

patterns_discovered.description: test_card(), test_app() helper functions construct minimal CardView and App instances for isolated unit tests
patterns_discovered.benefit: Decouples tests from filesystem I/O and complex App initialization

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Selection index preservation needs both preservation AND clamping
gotchas_discovered.symptom: If old column had selection at index 5 and new column has 3 cards, index 5 becomes invalid
gotchas_discovered.resolution: Use old_idx.min(new_col.cards.len() - 1) to clamp; handle empty column case separately (select(None))

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Wrapping navigation with negative steps requires rem_euclid, not % operator
gotchas_discovered.symptom: Plain % in Rust with negative operands gives negative results; h from index 0 in 3-column layout yields -1 instead of 2
gotchas_discovered.resolution: Use (candidate + step).rem_euclid(len as isize) to handle negative wraps correctly

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: All columns collapsed edge case needs explicit fallback
gotchas_discovered.symptom: Loop to find non-collapsed column exhausts all candidates; col_focus movement fails silently
gotchas_discovered.resolution: After loop, col_focus stays at current index (implicit no-op) rather than panicking or wrapping incorrectly

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: ListState selection defaults to None, not 0
gotchas_discovered.symptom: Pressing j/k on a column with no selection unwraps to Some(0), jumping to first card; expected no-op
gotchas_discovered.resolution: Use unwrap_or(0) to initialize; None is a valid state for unselected column

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Test KeyEvent construction requires explicit Kind and State enums
gotchas_discovered.symptom: Can't construct KeyEvent with shorthand; requires KeyEventKind::Press, KeyEventState::NONE
gotchas_discovered.resolution: Created test helper key() function to reduce boilerplate across 13 tests

approach_rationale: Separated concerns: app.rs handles state mutations, input.rs handles key dispatch and navigation logic
Test-driven: 13 unit tests validate wrapping, selection updates, and collapsed column behavior before wiring into event loop
Future-proof: placeholder handlers for Filter, ActionPopup, Detail modes allow this commit to land without breaking other subtasks
Preserved UX invariants: selection clamping, collapsed-column skipping, and 'stay-put-on-all-collapsed' fallback all protect against invalid states

recommendations.recommendation: Establish a shared test utilities module (e.g. ui/test_helpers.rs) to avoid duplication of test_card(), test_app(), key() across future input tests for Filter, ActionPopup, etc.

As other modes are wired (subtasks 4-1, 4-2, 4-3, 5-1, 5-2), ensure they follow the same wrapping + skip-collapsed pattern for consistency. Consider extracting move_col_focus and move_card_selection into shared utilities if reused by Detail or ActionPopup mode.

Add a property test or invariant checker: after any App mutation (refresh_columns, card movement via Shift+H/L in later subtasks), assert that col_focus < columns.len(), selected index < focused_column.cards.len(), and collapsed matches cards.is_empty(). Consider a validate_invariants() debug helper.

Current logic skips collapsed columns during navigation but does not render them (they collapse to 3-char glyphs per spec 029). When implementing visual rendering, verify that collapsed column glyphs are keyboard-accessible via Shift+H/L (card movement keybinds not yet wired), or consider allowing arrow key focus on glyph as a preview.

Current tests focus on Normal mode h/j/k/l. Once other modes (Detail, ActionPopup, etc.) are wired, add integration tests that switch modes and verify handlers are called correctly. Example: enter Detail mode, press keys, verify no navigation occurs.
